blocks.text:
Final Design

My final design is crafted in the distinctive style of David Carson, serving as a concise biography poster. Inspired by Carson’s artistic nuances, I incorporated signature elements to pay homage and infuse the design with his unique identity. The deliberate choice of a specific shape echoes Carson’s aesthetic preferences, symbolizing his impact on the design community. This design goes beyond imitation, offering a thoughtful interpretation of Carson’s style, becoming a visual celebration of his legacy and introducing newcomers to his creative imprint. It stands as a testament to the transformative influence of David Carson on my creative journey, merging his style with my own for a compelling biography poster.

Graphic Designer History Poster Design

In this project, I researched three graphic designers, carefully choosing one based on their impact and resonance. The challenge was to distill their story into a captivating visual narrative for a poster. Meticulously selecting elements like color schemes and typography, the poster became a storytelling medium reflecting the essence of the designer. It aimed to engage both enthusiasts and newcomers, showcasing the fusion of research, design, and storytelling. The resulting poster not only honored the chosen designer’s legacy but also represented my evolving connection to visual communication.

/graphic-design/project-six-6f87e-hep2b.detailTitle:
Soft Hands

Concept Album Packaging & Poster Design

blocks.text:
This project is a conceptual album and poster design created for a design assignment exploring the relationship between art and typography. The artist name and album title are entirely fictional and were created by me for this project. All visuals, typography, and design elements were also developed by me from concept to final layout.

I designed a full visual system including an album cover, tracklist layout, and promotional poster. The project focuses on mood and atmosphere, using soft light, gradients, and minimal typography to create a calm and emotional tone that reflects the imagined music style behind the album.

Skills: Visual Identity, Typography, Layout, Art Direction
Deliverables: Album Cover, Poster, Tracklist Layout
Tools: Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Illustrator

Portrait Poster Design

In this project, I undertook the task of designing a poster for my favorite star, Leonardo DiCaprio, employing various techniques in four distinct designs. The first design aimed to emulate a magazine cover, showcasing only his photo, accompanied by brief information. The second design adopted a simpler style, featuring a single photo and placing emphasis on his name, thereby presenting a spectrum of design styles ranging from simplicity to complexity. The third design took a more intricate approach by utilizing a cut-and-paste technique on different parts of his face, particularly the nose and mouth. This method added design features to maintain visual interest and prevent monotony. The incorporation of diverse design techniques in each poster provides a comprehensive and dynamic visual exploration of the iconic celebrity, offering viewers a nuanced and engaging representation that spans various artistic styles.

Nature Poster Design

In this nature poster project showcasing Mount Everest, I explored diverse design techniques across four distinct designs. The chosen approach for one design involved overlaying images, amplifying the visual experience and creating a dynamic composition. Another design embraced simplicity, featuring a single image of the mountain with its name, aiming for an elegant and straightforward visual impact. For the third design, I maintained a clean and simple aesthetic while integrating additional information about the location. This design not only serves as a visual representation but also becomes an informative piece, offering viewers a glimpse into the details and significance of Mount Everest. The project as a whole reflects a versatile exploration of design techniques, catering to various visual preferences and delivering a well-rounded representation of the iconic natural landmark.

In conceptualizing this work, I crafted a self-titled poster with a unique approach. The key design element involves utilizing various colors within the letters of my name. The deliberate choice of different colors serves the purpose of introducing a dynamic visual effect through overlapping, creating a sense of dimension akin to a 3D image. This intentional use of color and layering aims to enhance the overall aesthetic, providing a vibrant and engaging representation of my name in a visually compelling manner.

In this engaging project, I delved into historical events from the specific year 1980, focusing on the pivotal moment of John Lennon’s passing. Tasked with creating a poster, I employed a creative approach by utilizing all the letters to intricately craft an image that vividly represented John Lennon. This design not only pays homage to the iconic figure but also serves as a poignant visual tribute, capturing the essence of the significant event. The meticulous use of letters adds a layer of symbolism and creativity, making the poster a compelling and thoughtful exploration of the historical event.

Font Design 1

In this abstract exploration, the convergence of various letters became a celebration of creativity unbounded by linguistic norms. The result is a captivating and thought-provoking visual journey that transcends the traditional confines of language, inviting viewers to delve into the boundless realm of abstract expression.

Font Design 2

In this creative pursuit, I embarked on the journey of crafting my own fonts and numbers, drawing inspiration from a diverse range of techniques gleaned from my coursework. Meticulously applying the principles learned, I delved into the art of typography with a focus on creating a unique and personalized visual language.

Initial Logo Design

In this design project, I am tasked with creating a logo that creatively merges the initials of the first name and the last name. The goal is to develop a visually appealing and meaningful representation by combining these initials into a cohesive and distinctive logo. This process requires a thoughtful approach to design, considering factors such as typography, symmetry, and the overall aesthetic harmony to ensure the logo effectively communicates the identity it represents. The resulting design will serve as a unique and personalized emblem, encapsulating the essence of the individual through the synergy of their initials.
Final
Sketch

Typographic Systems Handbook

This comprehensive collection features diverse typography systems designed across various formats. The culmination of these works is meticulously compiled into a handbag, which, too, is a product of my design. This ensemble reflects not only my versatility in crafting typographic systems but also my ability to integrate them seamlessly into tangible, functional items. The handbag becomes a canvas that harmoniously showcases the amalgamation of my typography design skills and product design sensibilities.

Calendar Design

I designed a four-month calendar, using diverse fonts and strategic element placement to ensure visual aesthetics, readability, and overall cohesion. The calendar reflects my creative exploration of different font styles to evoke varied moods and enhance thematic elements, resulting in a harmonious and functional layout that integrates design principles seamlessly.

Branding Design

This project involves the creative process of designing a logo and establishing a personalized guitar product brand. Inspired by a passion for guitars, I aim to craft meticulously designed products under my brand. The challenge includes creating a visually compelling logo and strategically conceiving a brand name that reflects the essence and values of my guitar products. The logo development incorporates musical motifs, diverse color palettes, and symbolic representations to capture the brand’s spirit. Simultaneously, the brand name is carefully chosen to convey authenticity and quality, creating a connection with potential customers. Overall, this project represents a fusion of artistic expression and entrepreneurial vision, translating my love for guitars into a brand with a compelling narrative. The finalized logo and brand name will serve as the visual and conceptual pillars, marking the culmination of my creative and entrepreneurial aspirations in the realm of guitar products

Logo Design

This final logo marks the completion of my meticulous design process, featuring a carefully crafted guitar referencing a real image in Illustrator. The fusion of this intricately drafted guitar with the chosen brand name results in a visually compelling and cohesive logo. Beyond serving as a visual identity for the products, the design reflects the essence of my passion for guitars. It seamlessly blends digital rendering with symbolic representation, showcasing dedication and craftsmanship invested in each guitar under the brand name.

Visual Hierachy Design

In this project, I took on the task of designing a poster that communicates the schedule and timing of an event. The creative process involved the development of three unique designs, each distinguished by its use of different colors and techniques. The choice of colors aimed to evoke specific moods or themes related to the event, while the diverse techniques added a layer of visual interest and creativity. Through this variation, the three designs catered to different preferences and effectively conveyed the event details in an engaging and visually appealing manner.

Year Poster Design 1

In this creative project, I took on the challenge of designing a poster that captures the essence of various events from the year 1980. Drawing inspiration from movie posters, the design narrative unfolds in a cinematic style, weaving together a visual story of the occurrences of that year. The distinctive feature of the poster lies in the prominent use of numbers from the year 1980, strategically placed to serve as a visual focal point. This deliberate choice not only adds a unique and eye-catching element to the design but also serves as an effective and immediate way for viewers to connect with the historical context. The result is a compelling and visually engaging poster that invites viewers to explore and reflect on the significant events that unfolded in 1980.
Sketch

Year Poster Design 2

In this intriguing project, I delved into the historical events of the year 1980, uncovering the notable release of the first Pac-Man game in an arcade cabinet. Motivated by this discovery, I set out to design three variations that would pay homage to this iconic moment. The distinctive aspect of each variation lies in the overall style, where I deliberately chose to experiment with different fonts and diverse arrangements of images. This creative decision not only adds visual interest to the designs but also reflects a nuanced exploration of how typography and image composition contribute to the overall aesthetic. Each variation serves as a unique visual narrative, encapsulating the significance of the Pac-Man game in 1980 through creative and stylistic choices.

Magazine Design

In this project, I designed a fashion magazine, infusing each page with creativity and giving it a distinct name. With a focus on the brand Balenciaga, driven by my admiration for its style, every page and cover features captivating visuals and insightful details about Balenciaga. This creates a comprehensive exploration of the brand’s influence in the fashion realm.

The magazine takes a unique twist on the last page, offering a brief history of fashion across different years as a captivating conclusion. This final touch provides readers with a broader perspective on the evolution of fashion. The magazine, as a whole, seamlessly combines contemporary style, brand appreciation, and historical insights, catering to the diverse interests of fashion enthusiasts.
Sketch

Logo Design

In this project, I took on the task of crafting a fresh and impactful logo for a shop named “BlackCoffee.” The creative process involved generating a total of four distinct designs, each with its unique elements and visual appeal. The aim was to capture the essence of the coffee shop while ensuring versatility and memorability in the logo designs. Considerations such as color schemes, typography, and graphic elements played a crucial role in achieving a cohesive and engaging brand representation. The exploration of multiple designs allowed for a thoughtful selection, ensuring that the finalized logo would effectively resonate with the brand identity and leave a lasting impression on customers.

Rebranding Coffeeblack

In this task, I undertook a thorough rebranding initiative for the coffee shop “BlackCoffee,” extending across various elements such as the logo and assorted designs. The objective was to revitalize the visual identity of the establishment, ensuring a cohesive and appealing brand image. The logo redesign aimed for a modern and distinctive look, setting the tone for the overall rebranding strategy. Various design elements, including signage, packaging, and promotional materials, were revamped to align with the updated brand aesthetic. The comprehensive rebranding effort sought to enhance the coffee shop’s market presence, create a memorable visual impression, and elevate the overall customer experience.

Kwickcook Meal Plan Kit

In this collaborative project with my classmates, we embarked on the creative process of conceptualizing and designing a groundbreaking product—a meal plan kit. Our innovative approach centers around packaging not only fresh food but also accompanying recipes inside the kit. This unique offering empowers buyers to select their preferred menu, bringing the joy of home cooking to a new level. Within the box, customers discover a thoughtfully curated combination of fresh ingredients and detailed recipes, ensuring a seamless and delightful cooking experience. The meal plan kit not only provides convenience but also inspires culinary exploration, making it an appealing choice for those seeking a hassle-free yet personalized cooking solution.

Postcards from the Future

My project is about remembering the rain in Bangkok when I was growing up. I loved how the rain made the busy city feel quiet and calm. I also talked to my dad, who grew up in Bangkok in the 1970s. He said the rain used to be fun, but it also caused floods and problems.

Now, because of climate change, Bangkok gets even more rain and floods. In the future, the city might be underwater. I made a postcard that shows a mix of old Thai culture and a new underwater world. It shows how we must take care of our planet, or we might lose the places we love.

Front (Picture Side)
The front of the postcard shows a simple white drawing on a green background. In the middle, there is a Thai temple inside a big dome. Around it, there are fish, sea plants, and palm trees. You can also see tall buildings and helicopters, now under the water. This picture shows how people now live under the sea, mixing old Thai culture with a new underwater world.

Back (Message Side)
The handwritten message says

Due to the massive rainfall in recent years, the once vibrant and colorful city I call home is now facing violent floods. People are losing their homes and livelihoods. Temples and skyscrapers are now submerged under a sea of our own mistakes. We now drown in the faults of our own past

7 Sins Design

This project involved using Illustrator to design a poster. The instructor instructed me to select one of the seven deadly sins, and I chose greed. I depicted this sin by incorporating images of money and portraying a person being attacked by a devil-like figure, symbolizing the consequences of living with the sin of greed.

This project is a poster series celebrating four women type designers: Veronika Burian, Julieta Ulanovsky, Zuzana Licko, and Pooja Saxena. Each poster focuses on the designer’s name and biography, using typography as the main visual element.

The design explores repetition, scale, and layering of letterforms to show how type can create both meaning and visual texture. The background is made from overlapping letters, while the large names create hierarchy and identity for each poster. The goal of the project was to connect research about each designer with a strong visual expression.

Skills: Typography, Layout, Poster Design
Tools: Adobe Illustrator, Adobe InDesign

POTRAIT OF THE SELFLESS SELF

This semester we learned that Buddhism teaches that the self is not fixed or permanent. Rather than something solid, the self is understood as something that forms through conditions, attention, and experience. In this sense, Buddhism offers the idea of a selfless self: a way of being that still moves through the world, but without clinging to identity as stable or owned. We studied meditation practices that help reduce attachment to desire and selfhood. At the same time, we read stories about individuals moving through suffering, repetition, and release. These works show that even if the self is illusory, its journey still matters. This photo book follows that idea. Instead of focusing on one person, it uses places, landscapes, and moments of travel as a portrait of a self in motion. The images do not try to define who the self is. They trace how it appears, shifts, pauses, and changes. Each image marks a stage of samsara such as refuge, desire, repetition, or release. The self here is present, but not centered, shaped by movement rather than form. All photographs were taken on a Pentax Espio 125M 35mm film camera during travel in Japan, the United States, and Australia. The softness and grain of film reflect impermanence. Nothing here is meant to feel finished. The images move forward slowly and invite the viewer to move with them. This book is meant to be experienced as a passage. It begins with shelter and form, moves through attachment and repetition, and gradually opens toward self liberation. There is no final ending, only a lighter way of moving.

Temperature Glow Lamp

Concept

Connecting nature and human experience through technology by using temperature (a natural part of the environment) and light (something we can see and feel). The goal is to create a simple and interactive system that helps people notice, understand, and connect with their surroundings in a clearer and more meaningful way.

How does your system work?

My system integrates a DHT22 temperature and humidity sensor, a TTP223B touch sensor, and a WS2812B LED strip controlled by an Arduino. Here’s how the system works:

Inputs : The DHT22 sensor provides real-time temperature readings.

The TTP223B touch sensor acts as a toggle switch to control the brightness of the LED strip.

Outputs : The WS2812B LED strip lights up and changes color based on the

temperature :

Blue (<20°C): Cold temperatures.

Green (20–30°C): Moderate temperatures.

Red (>30°C): Hot temperatures.

The brightness of the LED strip changes incrementally with each touch of the TTP223B sensor.
https://pongsant.github.io/website/index.html

Web Design & Coding

I learned how to code using p5.js and how to make websites using HTML and CSS in Visual Studio Code. I used these skills to create my own website, where I showed all of my p5.js projects. I did all the design and coding, making sure the website looked good and worked well.

While working on this project, I learned more about interactive design, how to organize web pages, and how to make websites look good on different devices. I also figured out how to add animations and interactive features using p5.js, which made the website more fun to use. This project helped me bring together my love for design and technology and made me better at web development.

Flower Installation

My installation is a flower-shaped design inspired by a traditional Thai ceremonial flower torch. A soft light shines from the middle of the flower, making it feel calm and gentle. The flower has two interactive features: a button that turns the light on and off, and a light sensor that notices when something is in front of it—this makes the flower move, like it’s reacting to someone nearby. I also used Arduino and wrote code to make a touch sensor that turns the light on when someone gently rubs or touches it.

When I made this flower, I started by drawing the shape and thinking about how to add both light and movement to make it more interesting. I learned how to connect and code a button, a light sensor, and a touch sensor so they could all work together. It was hard at first, but also fun to figure out!

This project helped me understand how sensors can make art respond to people. I also practiced fixing problems and changing the code to make everything work better. It taught me how important it is to keep testing and improving, because every small change brought me closer to what I imagined.

/coding/the-last-hope.detailTitle: The Last Light — Interactive Flower Lamp

blocks.text:
The Last Light is an interactive project that connects a real object with a digital world. I built a flower-shaped lamp using Arduino, a distance sensor, and a servo motor. When someone walks close to the lamp, the sensor detects them and the flower slowly opens its petals. A warm light turns on inside, making it feel like the flower is quietly blooming.

At the same time, a screen nearby shows a 3D city that I created in Unity. The city looks damaged and empty, like a place after war and pollution. In the center of this world, the same glowing flower appears. Even though the world is broken, the flower is still alive. It becomes a symbol of hope, peace, and gentle life.

The project invites people to move slowly, notice the reaction of the flower, and reflect on how something small and beautiful can survive in a dark environment. It mixes physical computing and digital art to create a calm and emotional experience.

Skills: Arduino, Unity, Interaction Design, Concept Development
Tools: Ultrasonic Sensor, Servo Motor, 3D Environment Design

Concepts

I designed a decorative box that uses Thai patterns inspired by traditional OTOP crafts, mixing art and nature. The top of the box has a pattern that looks like flickering flames, bringing energy and liveliness to the design. The sides of the box have floral patterns that feel calm and peaceful, like flowers gently moving in the wind. Together, these designs create a nice balance between energy and stillness, showing the beauty of nature and Thai culture in a simple, modern way.

Final

This project was a valuable learning experience that helped me improve my woodworking skills and explore the blend of traditional Thai patterns with modern technology. I designed and built a decorative plywood box inspired by OTOP crafts, learning to use woodworking tools like saws, drills, and sanders. Initially challenging, I grew more confident as I practiced. Careful planning and precise measurements were crucial to ensuring the box fit together correctly.

The most exciting part was engraving Thai patterns using a laser cutter, merging cultural heritage with contemporary techniques. Adjusting the laser settings to achieve clean designs was both challenging and rewarding. This project deepened my appreciation for the balance between traditional craftsmanship and modern tools, reinforcing the importance of patience, detail, and thoughtful execution. Ultimately, it strengthened my connection to Thai culture and the joy of creating something meaningful.

Interactive Table

Our group project tackled loneliness by designing an interactive table with chess and tic-tac-toe engraved on its flip-top surface. I focused on the table’s design, using plywood for durability and engraving game patterns for a stylish, functional look. The legs symbolized modern connections, while matching stools with phone pockets encouraged face-to-face interactions.

We balanced aesthetics with functionality to make the table welcoming and accessible for public spaces. This project showed how design and teamwork can bring people together, proving that good design enhances real-world connections.
Sketch
Final
